idea.hook: How small is Mars's moon Phobos?
facts: Phobos, Mars's largest moon, is an irregular, potato-shaped rock.
Its average diameter is only about 22.5 kilometers (14 miles).
If you could drive a car at a leisurely 10 km/h (6 mph) across its surface...
idea.payoff: ...you could circumnavigate the entire moon in less than 7.5 hours!
idea.title: You Could DRIVE Around THIS Moon! 🚗🌕

scenes.text: How small is Mars's moon Phobos?
scenes.visual: A stylized animation of Mars in the background, with a tiny Phobos orbiting it. Camera slowly zooms out to emphasize the smallness of Phobos.
A detailed 3D render of Phobos slowly rotating, highlighting its irregular, non-spherical shape. Stars twinkle in the background.
Phobos shown next to a graphic representation of a small city or town (e.g., 14 miles across) for scale. A measurement line across Phobos indicates its diameter.

scenes.text: Drive a car at 10 km/h (6 mph) across its surface...
scenes.visual: An animated rover-like car driving slowly across a rugged, cratered surface resembling Phobos. A speedometer graphic shows '10 km/h'.
The car animation speeds up, showing it quickly completing a full loop around the entire Phobos surface. A digital clock displays '7:29' and then quickly ticks down to '<7.5 hours' with a flourish.

youtube.title: You Could DRIVE Around THIS Moon! 🚗🌕
